social.flavor: People rarely lie outright. They omit, redirect, and let you draw the conclusions they want you to draw. Finding the gap between what someone says and what they know takes patience and a very specific kind of attention.

social.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op)(op)+: If you succeed, your target suffers 2 Strife per (op)(op) spent this way.

social.name: Two Heads are Better than One
social.flavor: Two people with identical experience give you one perspective. Two people with different experience give you something more useful: options neither of them would have found alone.

social.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op)(op): Choose one skill in which you have 0 ranks and your target has 1 or more ranks. When you grant assistance to another character, you count as trained in that skill. This effect persists until the end of the scene.

street.name: Clean Hands
street.flavor: Anyone can start a fight. The skill is in finishing one lethally while walking away looking like the victim. Provoke the right response, let them throw the first punch, and by the time the body is cold, every witness in the room saw an accident.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op)+: Each observer with Vigilance equal to or less than the amount of (op) you spend this way cannot attest to lethal intent. Any Supporting evidence those observers would contribute to an investigation speaks only to the act itself, not to premeditation or malice. For the purposes of any Accusation Check, the crime is treated as a lesser charge (manslaughter rather than murder, assault rather than attempted homicide) unless other evidence of intent exists.

street.name: Field Recon
street.flavor: Knowledge of the terrain is a force multiplier. A scout who can quickly assess the environment and identify advantageous positions can turn the landscape into an ally, finding cover, chokepoints, and escape routes that others might miss.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op)(op) (Downtime or Mass Battle): Choose one area or battle zone within range of your survey. That area gains one of the following terrain qualities of your choice: Dangerous, Entangling, or Obscuring.

(op)(op) (Other Scene): Choose a position within the area you scouted. All terrain within 3 range bands of that position gains one of the following terrain qualities of your choice: Dangerous, Entangling, or Obscuring.
(op)+: You identify the precise location and composition of enemy forces in the area whose commanders have vigilance lower than your ranks in Survival plus (op) spent this way.

(op)(op)(op) (Mass Battle): On your next turn, if you target an enemy unit occupying a fortification, that unit does not benefit from the fortification's defensive properties against your action, and you may resolve your effects as if you were inside the fortification.

street.name: Friends in Low Places
street.flavor: In the shadows of the city, information is currency. A well-connected operator can tap into a network of informants, spies, and contacts to gain insights that others can't access. The right piece of information at the right time can be more valuable than any weapon.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Fortune (op): If you succeed, choose one skill. The informant has a number of ranks in that skill equal to your ranks in Subterfuge and can perform checks with that skill to assist you until the end of the scene.

street.name: Improvised Hazard
street.flavor: Ideal conditions mean overwhelming force, clear sightlines, and perfect positioning. Ideal conditions are rare. A smart operative learns to use whatever is at hand: broken glass cuts feet, a kicked-over shelf forces a detour, and a cloud of dust blinds just as well as any deployment.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op)(op): Choose one additional terrain quality (Dangerous, Entangling, or Obscuring) to apply to the terrain.

street.name: Phantom Step
street.flavor: Agility helps. So does choosing the right moment to move: the instant a guard looks away, the second a crowd surges, the beat when every eye is somewhere else. The best don't just move quietly. They move invisibly, and the difference is knowing when to go.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Swiftness (op): If you are in Obscuring terrain, within a crowd, or otherwise concealed from direct line of sight, choose one character whose vigilance is equal to or lower than your ranks in Stealth. That character loses track of your position. They cannot target you with Attack or Scheme actions until they succeed on an Insight check with TN equal to your ranks in Stealth to reacquire you, or until you act in a way that reveals your position.

street.name: Poison Blade
street.flavor: A blade is a weapon in itself, but it can also be a delivery system. A poisoned blade can strike with the lethality of both the toxin and the steel, and the victim may not even realize they've been hit until it's too late.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Precision (op): If you succeed,  the target suffers a critical strike with severity 2 plus 1 per (op) spent this way.
Precision (op)(op): If you succeed, the target is unaware of the substance until they suffer the first adverse effect caused by it.

street.name: Scrub
street.flavor: The difference between a crime and a rumor is documentation. Physical traces, access logs, witness memories: each can be managed or disposed of.

street.name: Alibi
street.flavor: In the world of espionage and covert operations, a well-constructed alibi can be the difference between a successful mission and a compromised one.

street.name: Dead Air
street.flavor: Most engagements fail at the moment of contact. The sound draws attention, the attention draws response, and the responders close the window. Cut the sound and the window stays open.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Precision (op)+: If you succeed, the target suffers a critical strike with severity equal to your weapon's deadliness plus 1 per (op) spent beyond the first.

street.name: Ghost Step
street.flavor: Elite operatives do not move around a building; they move through it. Walls, gaps, locked gates, and uneven debris are not obstacles to someone who treats the entire environment as a route. The best make it look effortless. They are simply somewhere else by the time anyone thinks to look.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op): Your movement produces no sound. You do not trigger detection checks based on sound or footfall during this movement.
Swiftness (op)(op): You may bring one willing character at range 0 with you, moving them the same distance along the same path.

street.name: Prepared Ambush
street.flavor: An ambush is not just about striking first. It's about controlling the moment of engagement, dictating the terms of the fight, and using the environment to your advantage. A well-prepared ambush can turn a dangerous encounter into a decisive victory.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Resilience (op)+: This effect persists for one additional round per (op) spent this way.
Resilience (op)+: Reduce the TN of the first Attack action check one concealed target makes against a character in the same terrain by 1 per (op) spent this way.

street.name: Silent Strike
street.flavor: There is no such thing as a fair fight. There is only the fight you walked into and the one you set up. A disoriented target, a suppressed barrel, and a clear angle: one of these is a circumstance. All three is a decision.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Precision (op)+: Increase the severity of the critical strike by 1 per (op) spent this way.
Precision (op)(op): The target gains the Silenced condition. Other characters at range 3 or greater do not perceive that the target has been struck for 1 round.

street.name: Volatile Dispersal
street.flavor: Not every weapon is built to kill outright. A well-deployed chemical payload does something more economical: it denies ground, degrades performance, and forces opponents to choose between breathing and fighting. Operators who understand chemistry understand that the most dangerous thing in a room does not always have a trigger.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Swiftness (op): Move 1 range band.
(op)+: The area at range 0–2 of your position gains the Obscuring and Dangerous terrain qualities until the end of the scene.
(op)+: One target per (op) spent this way suffers a critical strike with severity 2.

street.name: What's Yours Is Mine
street.flavor: A skilled thief can take more than just a target's possessions. They can take their sense of security, their confidence, and even their breath. A well-timed strike can leave an opponent not just empty-handed, but vulnerable in every way.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Power (op): The target suffers the Dazed condition.
Power (op)+: Move 1 range band, plus 1 additional range band per (op) spent beyond the first.
Power (op)(op)+: If you succeed, you may take one additional item per (op)(op) spent this way.

street.name: Call the Wild
street.flavor: An animal companion can be more than just a pet. With the right training and bond, they can become a fierce ally in combat, capable of turning the tide of battle with their strength and ferocity.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op)+: You may target one additional friendly animal companion per (op) spent this way.
(op)+: The target animal removes 1 Fatigue per (op) spent this way.

street.name: Silent Assassin
street.flavor: There is a category of professional beyond deadly: the kind you only discover after the fact. No raised alarm. No struggle. Just stillness, and then a room full of bodies, each taken so cleanly that the last one never knew the first was already gone.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op)+: Increase the TN of the next check any one character makes to resist a critical strike you inflict by 1 per (op) spent this way. This effect persists until the end of your next turn.

street.name: Vanishing Act
street.flavor: The best way to win a fight is to avoid it entirely. A skilled operator can use the environment to disappear from sight, slipping away from danger and leaving their opponents grasping at shadows.

street.name: Activation:
opportunities: Swiftness (op)+ (Mass Battle): If you succeed, each enemy unit with forces inside the terrain suffers a morale disruption; their commander must make a TN equal to (op) spent this way Command check or that unit suffers a penalty to its next order.
Swiftness (op)+ (Other Scene): If you succeed, each character you did not target who is within the terrain receives 1 strife per (op) spent this way.

(op)(op)(op): Even if you fail, increase the TN of Attack action checks targeting each of your targets by 2. This effect persists until any of your targets performs an Attack action or leaves the terrain.

vehicle.name: Bumper Tag
vehicle.flavor: Two tons of steel at speed is a statement. The best way to deliver it is through a rear bumper. Physics does the rest.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Power (op): Your vehicle suffers 2 less damage from the collision.
Power (op)(op): The target driver suffers the Disoriented condition.

vehicle.name: Dirty Windshield
vehicle.flavor: A clean line of sight is a gift from your opponent. Gravel, dust, water, debris. The road is full of material that can be weaponized with a hard turn and the right application of throttle.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Resilience (op): The terrain persists for one additional round.
Resilience (op)(op): The terrain also becomes Dangerous terrain. Characters entering the area must make a TN 2 Survival or Piloting check each round or suffer the Dazed condition.

vehicle.name: Drive-By Directive
vehicle.flavor: The wheel in one hand, a sidearm in the other. Most people can manage one of those tasks competently. Doing both at speed requires a different kind of coordination, the kind earned by running enough pursuits to lose count.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: (op): Ignore 1 point of the target's physical resistance from vehicle armor.
(op)(op): Move 1 range band before or after resolving the attack without spending an action.

vehicle.name: J-Turn
vehicle.flavor: Everyone expects the pursued to keep running. A J-turn exploits that assumption. You spin and face them. The drivers who were already falling behind see the move develop and round on you at their leisure. The aggressive ones eat two blocks of overshoot.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Swiftness (op): You may immediately Accelerate. If you do, scene speed increases from the halved value as normal.
Swiftness (op)(op): Gain 1 momentum on any objective involving escape or evasion.

vehicle.name: Lead Foot
vehicle.flavor: Most drivers flinch when the needle climbs. You lean in. The engine has more to give and your foot already knows the question.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Swiftness (op): The scene speed increases by 2 instead of 1.
Swiftness (op)(op): Until the start of your next turn, increase the TN of techniques targeting you by 1.

vehicle.name: Rapid Transit
vehicle.flavor: Efficiency in movement is a tactical advantage. An operator who knows how to optimize routes, anticipate obstacles, and maintain momentum can cover ground faster than anyone expects, turning distance into a manageable variable rather than a constraint.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Fortune (op): You may lead a number of willing characters with vehicles of their own up to your ranks in Piloting.

vehicle.name: Chicken
vehicle.flavor: Two vehicles on a collision course. Whoever blinks loses ground. If neither does, both cars eat the closing speed. The one with the heavier frame walks away less broken.

vehicle.name: PIT Maneuver
vehicle.flavor: The rear quarter panel of a vehicle is a lever. One tap in the right place spins the target with its own momentum. Physics does not give them a choice.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Precision (op)(op): If the effect would cause the target to reach 3 or more stacks of Losing Ground, they are instead violently knocked out. All occupants suffer a Critical Strike with severity equal to the current scene speed.

vehicle.name: Ride the Line
vehicle.flavor: Every driver has a limit. Most discover it by crossing it. Skill helps you stay on the road. Nerve keeps your foot in the throttle when every instinct says brake.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Fortune (op)(op): Treat the failed check as if it succeeded with 0 bonus successes instead. This technique can still only be applied once per failed check.

vehicle.name: Rolling Fortress
vehicle.flavor: A vehicle parked between your team and incoming fire is better than any wall you could build in the same time. Steel body panels and an engine block absorb rounds that would otherwise find flesh.

vehicle.name: Thread the Needle
vehicle.flavor: Most drivers see a gap and wonder if they fit. You see the same gap and wonder if you need to slow down. Spatial awareness at speed is training, paid for in paint transfers and close calls.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Swiftness (op)+: One pursuing vehicle per (op) spent this way cannot follow you through the gap and must find an alternate route, gaining 1 stack of Losing Ground.
Swiftness (op)(op): You may immediately perform a second Movement action without spending an action.

vehicle.name: Ghost Ride
vehicle.flavor: Sometimes the vehicle is the weapon and you are the delivery system. Lock the wheel. Pick the line. Jump. Two tons of steel at speed does not care about armor.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Fortune (op): You may aim the vehicle toward a specific target you can perceive, extending its path to reach them if they are within 3 range bands.
Fortune (op)(op): The vehicle's wreckage counts as Dangerous and Obscuring terrain at its final position until the end of the scene.

vehicle.name: Push It to the Limit
vehicle.flavor: The manufacturer put a redline on the tachometer. You have never respected it. The engine screams, the frame shakes, and the gap changes. Whether it closes or widens depends on which side of the chase you are on.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Resilience (op): Reduce the damage your vehicle suffers from this technique by 2.
Resilience (op)(op): You do not suffer the strife cost from this technique (the vehicle damage remains).

vehicle.name: Smoke and Mirrors
vehicle.flavor: A garage entrance. An off-ramp. A tunnel mouth. The best escapes start with a hard turn into somewhere the pursuers did not expect you to go. By the time they realize what happened, the gap has stretched from meters to memory.

vehicle.name: Totaled
vehicle.flavor: There are clean ways to end a chase: a spike strip, a barricade, coordinated radio traffic. Then there is the way that ends with a fireball and paperwork. You are the paperwork kind of problem. A vehicle is a projectile. All it needs is the right trajectory.

vehicle.name: Activation:
opportunities: Power (op)+: Reduce the damage your vehicle suffers by 1 per (op) spent this way.
